classes.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.action
documentation:
The name of the action currently running, or the id of a step.

GitHub removes special characters, and uses the name `__run` when the current step
runs a script without an id.
If you use the same action more than once in the same job,
the name will include a suffix with the sequence number with underscore before it.

For example, the first script you run will have the name` __run`,
and the second script will be named `__run_2`.
Similarly, the second invocation of `actions/checkout` will be `actionscheckout2`.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.action_path
documentation:
The path where an action is located. This property is only supported in composite actions.

You can use this path to access files located in the same repository as the action,
for example by changing directories to the path.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.action_ref
documentation: For a step executing an action, this is the ref of the action being executed. For example, `v2`.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.action_repository
documentation:
For a step executing an action, this is the owner and repository name of the action being executed.
For example, `actions/checkout`.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.action_status
documentation: For a composite action, the current result of the composite action.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.actor
documentation:
The username of the user that triggered the initial workflow run.

If the workflow run is a re-run, this value may differ from `triggering_actor`.
Any workflow re-runs will use the privileges of `actor`,
even if the actor initiating the re-run (`triggering_actor`) has different privileges.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.actor_id
documentation: The account ID of the person or app that triggered the initial workflow run, e.g., 1234567.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.api_url
documentation: The URL of the GitHub REST API.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.base_ref
documentation:
The base reference (i.e., target branch) of the pull request in a workflow run.

This property is only available when the event that triggers a workflow run
is either pull_request or pull_request_target.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.env
documentation:
Path on the runner to the file that sets environment variables from workflow commands.

This file is unique to the current step and is a different file for each step in a job.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.event

returns.name: IssueCommentPayload | IssuesPayload | PullRequestPayload | PushPayload | SchedulePayload | WorkflowDispatchPayload | Payload

documentation:
The full event webhook payload.

This is identical to the webhook payload of the event that triggered the workflow run,
and is different for each event.
The webhooks for each GitHub Actions event is linked in
[Events that trigger workflows](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/events-that-trigger-workflows).
For example, for a workflow run triggered by the [push event](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/events-that-trigger-workflows#push),
this object contains the contents of the [push webhook payload](https://docs.github.com/en/webhooks-and-events/webhooks/webhook-events-and-payloads#push).

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.event_name
documentation: The event type that triggered the workflow.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.event_path
documentation: The path to the file on the runner that contains the full event webhook payload.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.graphql_url
documentation: The URL of the GitHub GraphQL API.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.head_ref
documentation:
The head reference (i.e., source branch) of the pull request in a workflow run.

This is only available when the event that triggers a workflow run is
either 'pull_request' or 'pull_request_target'.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.job
documentation:
The [`job_id`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions#jobsjob_id)
of the current job.

This is set by the Actions runner, and is only available within the execution steps of a job.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.path
documentation:
Path on the runner to the file that sets system PATH variables from workflow commands.

This file is unique to the current step and is a different file for each step in a job.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.ref
documentation:
The fully formed reference of the branch or tag that triggered the workflow run,
e.g. 'refs/heads/main', 'refs/tags/v1.0' etc.

Notes
-----
- For workflows triggered by push, this is the branch or tag ref that was pushed.
- For workflows triggered by pull_request, this is the pull request merge branch.
- For workflows triggered by release, this is the release tag created.
- For other triggers, this is the branch or tag ref that triggered the workflow run.
- This is only set if a branch or tag is available for the event type.
- The ref given is fully-formed, meaning that for branches the format is `refs/heads/<branch_name>`,
  for pull requests it is `refs/pull/<pr_number>/merge`,
  and for tags it is `refs/tags/<tag_name>`.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.ref_name
documentation:
The short reference name of the branch or tag that triggered the event.

Notes
-----
- This value matches the branch or tag name shown on GitHub, e.g., `main`, `dev/1`.
- For pull requests, the format is `refs/pull/<pr_number>/merge`.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.ref_protected
documentation: Whether the branch or tag that triggered the workflow run is protected.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.ref_type
documentation: The type of the ref that triggered the event, either 'branch' or 'tag'.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.repository
documentation:
Full name of the repository, i.e. `<owner_username>/<repo_name>`,
e.g., 'RepoDynamics/RepoDynamics'.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.repository_id
documentation: The ID of the repository, e.g., `123456789`.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.repository_owner
documentation: GitHub username of the repository owner.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.repository_owner_id
documentation: The account ID of the repository owner, e.g., `1234567`.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.repository_url
documentation: The Git URL of the repository, e.g., `git://github.com/RepoDynamics/GitHub-Contexts.git`.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.retention_days
documentation: The number of days that workflow run logs and artifacts are kept.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.run_id
documentation:
A unique number for each workflow run within a repository.

This number does not change when the workflow is re-run.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.run_number
documentation:
A unique number for each run of a particular workflow in a repository.

This number begins at 1 for the workflow's first run,
and increments with each new run.
This number does not change when the workflow is re-run.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.run_attempt
documentation:
A unique number for each attempt of a particular workflow run in a repository.

This number begins at 1 for the workflow run's first attempt, and increments with each re-run.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.secret_source
documentation: The source of a secret used in a workflow.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.server_url
documentation: The URL of the GitHub server, e.g., `https://github.com`.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.sha
documentation:
The SHA hash of the most recent commit on the branch that triggered the workflow.

The value of this commit SHA depends on the event that triggered the workflow.
For more information, see References.

References
----------
- [GitHub Docs: Events that trigger workflows](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/events-that-trigger-workflows)

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.token
documentation:
A token to authenticate on behalf of the GitHub App installed on your repository.

This is functionally equivalent to the `GITHUB_TOKEN` secret.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.triggering_actor
documentation:
The username of the user that initiated the workflow run.

If the workflow run is a re-run, this value may differ from `actor`.
Any workflow re-runs will use the privileges of `actor`,
even if the actor initiating the re-run (`triggering_actor`) has different privileges.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.workflow
documentation:
The name of the workflow.

If the workflow file doesn't specify a name,
the value of this property is the full path of the workflow file in the repository.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.workflow_ref
documentation:
The ref path to the workflow,
e.g., `octocat/hello-world/.github/workflows/my-workflow.yml@refs/heads/my_branch`.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.workflow_sha
documentation: The commit SHA hash of the workflow file.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.workspace
documentation:
The default working directory on the runner for steps,
and the default location of your repository when using the
[checkout action](https://github.com/actions/checkout).

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.repository_name
documentation: Name of the repository, e.g., `GitHub-Contexts`.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.hash_before
documentation: The SHA hash of the most recent commit on the branch before the event.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.context.GitHubContext.hash_after
documentation: The SHA hash of the most recent commit on the branch after the event.

The 'github' context of the workflow run.

It contains information about the workflow run and the event that triggered the run.

References
----------
- [GitHub Docs](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts#github-context)

methods.name: github_contexts.github.payload.base.Payload.enterprise

An enterprise on GitHub.

This is only available when the webhook is configured on an enterprise account
or an organization that's part of an enterprise account.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.payload.base.Payload.installation

The GitHub App installation.

This is only available when the event is configured for and sent to a GitHub App.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.payload.base.Payload.organization

An organization on GitHub.

This is only available when the event occurs from activity in a repository owned by an organization,
or when the webhook is configured for an organization.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.payload.base.Payload.repository
documentation:
The repository on GitHub where the event occurred.

This is only available when the event occurs from activity in the repository.

methods.name: github_contexts.github.payload.base.Payload.sender
documentation: The GitHub user that triggered the event.

The full webhook payload of the triggering event.

References
----------
- [GitHub Docs](https://docs.github.com/en/webhooks/webhook-events-and-payloads)

classes.name: github_contexts.github.enum.ActionType
documentation:
Triggering actions of events that can trigger a workflow.
Each action is only available for certain events.

Attributes
----------
ASSIGNED : str
    An issue or pull request was assigned to a user.
    Available for `issues`, `pull_request`.
AUTO_MERGE_DISABLED : str
    Auto merge was disabled for a pull request.
    Available for `pull_request`.
AUTO_MERGE_ENABLED : str
    Auto merge was enabled for a pull request.
    Available for `pull_request`.
CLOSED : str
    An issue or pull request was closed.
    Available for `issues`, `pull_request`.
CONVERTED_TO_DRAFT : str
    A pull request was converted to a draft.
    Available for `pull_request`.
CREATED : str
    A comment on an issue or pull request was created.
    Available for `issue_comment`.
DELETED : str
    An issue, pull request, or a comment on an issue or pull request was deleted.
    Available for `issue_comment`, `issues`, `pull_request`.
DEMILESTONED : str
    An issue or pull request was removed from a milestone.
    Available for `issues`, `pull_request`.
DEQUEUED : str
    A pull request was removed from the merge queue.
    Available for `pull_request`.
EDITED : str
    The title or body on an issue or pull request,
    or a comment on an issue or pull request was edited,
    or the base branch of a pull request was changed.
    Available for `issue_comment`, `issues`, `pull_request`.
ENQUEUED : str
    A pull request was added to the merge queue.
    Available for `pull_request`.
LABELED : str
    A label was added to an issue or pull request.
    Available for `issues`, `pull_request`.
LOCKED : str
    Conversation on an issue or pull request was locked.
    Available for `issues`, `pull_request`.
MILESTONED : str
    An issue or pull reqeust was added to a milestone.
    Available for `issues`, `pull_request`.
OPENED : str
    An issue or pull request was created.
    Available for `issues`, `pull_request`.
PINNED : str
    An issue was pinned to a repository.
    Available for `issues`.
READY_FOR_REVIEW : str
    A draft pull request was marked as ready for review.
    Available for `pull_request`.
REOPENED : str
    A closed issue or pull request was reopened.
    Available for `issues`, `pull_request`.
REVIEW_REQUEST_REMOVED : str
    A request for review by a person or team was removed from a pull request.
    Available for `pull_request`.
REVIE_REQUESTED : str
    Review by a person or team was requested for a pull request.
    Available for `pull_request`.
SYNCHRONIZE : str
    A pull request's head branch was updated.
    For example, the head branch was updated from the base branch
    or new commits were pushed to the head branch.
    Available for `pull_request`.
TRANSFERRED : str
    An issue was transferred to another repository.
    Available for `issues`.
UNASSIGNED : str
    A user was unassigned from an issue or pull request.
    Available for `issues`, `pull_request`.
UNLABELED : str
    A label was removed from an issue or pull request.
    Available for `issues`, `pull_request`.
UNLOCKED : str
    Conversation on an issue or pull request was unlocked.
    Available for `issues`, `pull_request`.
UNPINNED : str
    An issue was unpinned from a repository.
    Available for `issues`.

classes.name: github_contexts.github.enum.SecretSource
documentation: The source of a secret used in a workflow.
